title: strychnos tomentosa
intro: Strychnos tomentosa, also known as the tree of death, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its dark green, elliptical leaves and distinctive, red fruit. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Strychnos tomentosa include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to the affected area.
